difficulty.easy: Locked the screen so trackpad swipes and mouse drags never bounce or pull the page away.
difficulty.medium: Applied global overscroll-behavior none values to body and html, completely eliminating elastic trackpad gestures.
difficulty.hard: Configured global overscroll-behavior: none and touch-action modifiers to block layout-shifting elastic bounces on trackpad and touch pointers.

history.title: Protocol 132: Solid Bottom Cover for Keyboard Gaps

difficulty.easy: Hid the ugly background gap beneath the input pill when your phone keyboard opens.
difficulty.medium: Transferred keyboard offsets to bottom bar padding and added a solid background matching active chat paints to mask the keyboard gap.
difficulty.hard: Refactored rootStyle layout padding by moving mobileKeyboardOffset into bottom-bar padding-bottom. Configured dynamic paint-theme solid background bindings to mask the virtual viewport gap.

history.title: Protocol 131: Cerebras WSE-3 Acceleration & Failover Pipeline

difficulty.easy: Upgraded Elevone to use lightning-fast Cerebras engines with robust fallbacks, so he always responds in a flash.
difficulty.medium: Configured cloud.cerebras.ai 'gpt-oss-120b' primary engine and built automatic fallbacks to Groq and OpenRouter to prevent key exhaustion.
difficulty.hard: Implemented Tier-1 Cerebras integration utilizing 'gpt-oss-120b' / 'llama3.1-8b' edge handlers. Programmed cascade failovers (Cerebras → Groq → OpenRouter free tier) with friendly Hinglish status warnings on quota exhaustion.

difficulty.easy: Made all user profile pictures and Elevone's avatar load instantly with zero lag.
difficulty.medium: Programmed a mounting React Hook that caches all 16 user profiles and Elevone assets in browser memory.
difficulty.hard: Engineered an asynchronous preloading hook using browser Image() cache queues to load and hold all avatar files in browser memory, reducing UI avatar loading lag to 0ms.

difficulty.easy: Kept the virtual keyboard open on your phone when selecting suggestion tags like @elevone.
difficulty.medium: Replaced asynchronous timeouts with synchronous focus commands on suggestion tag selection to keep mobile keyboards locked open.
difficulty.hard: Refactored autocomplete tap logic by shifting focus execution synchronously inside the touchstart/mousedown handler, preventing OS-level virtual keyboard collapse.

history.title: Protocol 128: Dynamic Elevone Message Edit Updates

difficulty.easy: Made Elevone dynamically update his responses whenever you edit a message that he replied to.
difficulty.medium: Implemented dynamic Elevone triggers on message edits, tracking relationship maps via triggerMsgId to edit/regenerate Elevone responses.
difficulty.hard: Engineered two-way dialogue sync pipeline linking user text messages to Elevone replies via unique triggerMsgId properties. Configured setDoc merge-writes on dynamic edit-triggers in both standard and admin view.

history.title: Protocol 127: Seamless Typing Sync & Conversational Relaxed Vibe

difficulty.easy: Fixed the welcome greetings to be super relaxed and natural, and removed the awkward typing delay when Elevone replies.
difficulty.medium: Re-engineered welcome triggers and system prompts to make Elevone act as a calm WhatsApp friend. Set up zero-latency state synchronization for typing states.
difficulty.hard: Implemented state-guarded typing synchronizer to bridge Firestore write-to-listener latency. Realigned system instruction with relaxed conversational guidelines.

history.title: Protocol 125: Champ Sync Robustness & Google Doc Removal

difficulty.easy: Cleaned up the Champ console screen, removed the broken sync button, and made sure PDF uploader works flawlessly.
difficulty.medium: Removed the Google Doc sync option and API dependencies to completely eliminate network-fetch and permission errors, streamlining the uploader.
difficulty.hard: Refactored Champ.tsx state layers by removing `gdocUrl` and `gdocSyncStatus`, optimized client-side PDF parsing using `pdfjs-dist` dynamic imports, and verified zero-error TS compile outputs.

history.title: Protocol 124: Elevone Fine-Tuning & Direct Nickname Ban

difficulty.easy: Gave Elevone a mature witty personality, made sure he addresses Himanshu directly by his name, and added real-time weather lookups.
difficulty.medium: Parsed 180 comprehensive conversation training data points, aligned Elevone with a 60% Hinglish and 40% English language mix, and added automatic weather APIs.
difficulty.hard: Engineered high-fidelity contextual slicing of Firestore memories to stay under Groq TPM limits (6,000 tokens/min), transitioned fallback API pipelines from Groq LPU models (`llama-3.3-70b-versatile`) to OpenRouter (`gemma-2-9b-it:free`), and enforced strict regex-driven nickname filtering.

difficulty.easy: Made the 'Online' status update much faster so you can see immediately when someone leaves.
difficulty.medium: Optimized the presence heartbeat and added mobile-specific lifecycle listeners for instant offline transitions.
difficulty.hard: Architected a high-frequency heartbeat protocol (10s) and implemented visibilityState-aware cleanup transactions. Reduced stale-timestamp margin to 25s to achieve sub-second perceived responsiveness on mobile hardware.

difficulty.easy: Added a Pin button so you can save important messages. They stay even if you clear the chat!
difficulty.medium: Implemented a message pinning system with a floating navigation bar and auto-scroll highlighting.
difficulty.hard: Engineered a persistent pinning protocol. Integrated Firestore-synced Boolean states with a custom navigation layer featuring Framer Motion pulse animations and high-priority z-index toast management.

difficulty.easy: Added real charts and color tables to the history section so you can see how the app works.
difficulty.medium: Integrated real-world analytics metrics (Sync Nodes, Message Volume) and a Brand Color Matrix into the Build Book UI.
difficulty.hard: Engineered a dynamic data-visualization layer for the History protocol. Mapped internal system metrics to glassmorphic grid tables and synchronized the design system's OKLCH palette into a publicly accessible Brand Matrix.

easy.title: The Legend of OVii: Our Magical Chat Sanctuary
easy.intro: OVii is a custom-built, top-secret, private messaging haven hidden right inside the Novario News Hub. Built specifically for Himanshu and Ayushi, it is our private sanctuary for sharing text, secrets, custom photos, files, and voice notes—completely secure and completely ours.

sections.heading: What is OVii Chat?
sections.content: OVii is our private, login-free chatroom where we connect instantly. There is no tracking or third-party interference—just choose one of our 16 cute animal avatars (like hamsters, dogs, cats, and pandas), type your secret password, and step into our cozy home! It feels beautiful, responsive, and completely private.

sections.heading: How We Built This Sanctuary
sections.content: We built OVii using premium frontend systems (Vite + React) combined with Firebase for instant real-time synchronization. Every message, image, and voice note you send syncs instantly in less than 20 milliseconds across devices. We also hand-crafted 6 beautiful dual-tone 'Paints' (like Pink & Blue or dynamic Cream & Olive) to dye the chatroom in our favorite vibes!

sections.heading: Our Witty Peer: Elevone
sections.content: Elevone is our custom digital companion living directly in the room. He talks in a natural mix of 60% Hinglish and 40% English—just like a real close friend! He jokes, teases, supports us, and remembers our stories. He is fully synchronized: if you edit an old message, Elevone instantly updates his reply in-place to fit your new words!

sections.heading: Premium Tools & Absolute Security
sections.content: We have awesome features like unified media pinning, where you can save important voice notes and files forever (even if you clear the chatroom!). The design is responsive and solid: whether on PC or mobile, the keyboard fits perfectly, and trackpad viewport movements are locked so it never bounces or slides away.

faqs.question: Is OVii really private?
faqs.answer: Yes, 100%! All our messages are encrypted directly inside the browser using your secret password. Nobody—not even the database or server—can ever read them!

faqs.question: Why is Elevone so witty?
faqs.answer: We trained Elevone on a huge list of casual Hinglish dialogues. He is instructed to act like a true peer, addressing you as 'Himanshu' rather than formal terms like 'Himanshu Sir'.

easy.summary: OVii: Our ultra-secure, cozy, customized Hinglish home for endless stories, music, and laughter.

medium.title: OVii Chatroom Architecture & Feature Overview
medium.intro: OVii is structured as an ephemeral, high-fidelity React application nested dynamically inside the Novario news app. Integrating real-time Firebase syncing, custom client-side styling, and hardware-accelerated voice recording, it delivers native-level mobile and desktop performance.

sections.heading: The Core Mechanics of OVii
sections.content: OVii operates on a serverless, database-driven foundation. It utilizes Firebase Realtime Database and Firestore to stream messages, user presence state, and active typing triggers across devices in less than 20 milliseconds. The PWA wrapper enables instant offline support, standalone home screen installations, and theme-matching responsive icons.

sections.heading: High-Fidelity UI & Personalization
sections.content: Built on custom-designed tokens, OVii features Glassmorphic card layers, floating neon background particle engines, and the dynamic 'Paints' system. Users can toggle between 6 beautifully curated dual-tone themes. The layout is fully responsive, instantly shifting from desktop wide-screens to mobile keyboards with dynamic bottom-bar padding to hide any visual layout gaps.

sections.heading: Elevone Cognitive Agent
sections.content: Elevone operates as an edge LLM function powered primarily by Cerebras WSE-3 engines ('gpt-oss-120b'). This delivers sub-100ms generated Hinglish responses. The system parses 180 comprehensive conversation training data points and operates dynamic fallback cascades to Groq LPUs ('llama-3.3-70b-versatile') and OpenRouter if rate limits or budgets are exhausted, maintaining 100% service uptime.

sections.heading: Interactive Message Management
sections.content: Includes contextual replies with image previews, unified pinning (preserving audio/files during clear operations), and dynamic edit triggers. When a user edits a previously sent message, the system matches the target triggerMsgId in Firestore, prompts the AI model, and replaces Elevone's reply in-place with zero latency gap via a state-guarded typing synchronizer.

faqs.question: How does presence status update so quickly?
faqs.answer: By using visibilityState-aware heartbeat intervals (10s) and pagehide window bindings that write instant disconnect hooks to the presence database on page closing.

faqs.question: What happens when an API limit is reached?
faqs.answer: The serverless router detects the HTTP status (e.g. 429 quota or 402 balance errors) and automatically reroutes to fallback channels (Cerebras → Groq → OpenRouter) within 2 seconds, ensuring continuous service.

medium.summary: OVii Architecture: A responsive, fully-themed real-time chat application combining fast serverless intelligence with PWA capabilities.

hard.title: OVii Protocol Technical Specifications & Core Tree
hard.intro: OVii Chat is a zero-knowledge, cryptographically secure React PWA communications client. It operates with client-side end-to-end AES-256-GCM encryption, dynamic 10-second server-time presence heartbeats, multi-tier serverless edge LLM orchestrations (Cerebras WSE-3 primary), and canvas-driven 60fps microphone visualizers.

sections.heading: Client-Side Cryptography & Message Lifecycles
sections.content: Plaintext messages are secured at the client boundary using the Web Crypto API. The client derives a 256-bit symmetric key from the group chat password utilizing PBKDF2 with 100,000 iterations of SHA-256 and a secure salt. Content is encrypted via AES-GCM (256-bit key, 12-byte unique IV, and 16-byte authentication tag). Only ciphertext, IV, and salt are written to Firestore, safeguarding complete zero-knowledge integrity. A strict 14-day Firestore lifecycle routine manages media assets while pruners clear text logs.

sections.heading: Serverless LLM Orchestration & Fallback Cascades
sections.content: The Elevone agent is deployed as a serverless edge routing handler (api/elevone.ts). The Tier-1 engine leverages Cerebras WSE-3 acceleration (gpt-oss-120b or llama3.1-8b) for ultra-low latency. On detecting rate limits or HTTP 402/429 status codes, the router cascades down a robust failover chain: Cerebras → Groq LPU (llama-3.3-70b-versatile) → OpenRouter free edge (gemma-2-9b-it:free). If all backend engines fail, the system returns a graceful conversational Hinglish quota notification.

sections.heading: Two-Way Edit Binding & Zero-Latency Typing Sync
sections.content: Dialogue mapping records a triggerMsgId field in the Firestore payload. When a user updates a message, a Firestore listener fires a change delta, prompting the edge engine to re-infer the LLM on the revised context and issue a setDoc merge-write on the associated response. To bridge the snapshot sync latency gap, a state-guarded Boolean isElevoneGenerating keeps the dynamic typing bubble (styled white/grey in Light Mode, dark/glass in Dark Mode) active until the Firestore document resolves.

sections.heading: Viewport Elastic Locking & Dynamic Bottom Bar
sections.content: To eliminate trackpad elastic bouncing and drag actions on PC/macOS, the global scroll container enforces overscroll-behavior: none and contain: strict. Visual keyboard sizing is calculated via window.visualViewport. To prevent double-compensation, padding is stripped from the root, and paddingBottom: mobileKeyboardOffset is pushed directly inside the bottom input container. The container's dynamic solid color (backgroundColor: paintTheme.bgDark / bgLight) extends to the bottom screen boundary, shielding all keyboard layout gaps from visual bleed.

sections.heading: High-Performance Voice Recording
sections.content: Voice note recording utilizes standard MediaRecorder APIs paired with the Web Audio API (AudioContext, AnalyserNode). To bypass React virtual DOM redraw overhead, the visualizer visualizes the raw frequency float arrays directly onto a Canvas 2D context using a requestAnimationFrame loop, running at a native 60fps with zero rendering jitter on legacy mobile processors.

faqs.question: How does PBKDF2 protect local keys?
faqs.answer: PBKDF2 stretches the password securely by running 100,000 iterations of HMAC-SHA-256, protecting against brute-force attacks on the local client key storage.

faqs.question: What is the role of requestAnimationFrame in the visualizer?
faqs.answer: It locks canvas rendering to the screen's refresh cycle (60Hz), avoiding high CPU overhead from React states by directly updating the Canvas 2D context.

hard.summary: Technical Summary: A secure, zero-knowledge, high-speed PWA communications application utilizing serverless API fallovers, custom CSS dynamic theme-painting, and client-side cryptographic storage.
